questions.question: What is the primary difference between importing Asana data into Notion versus using Synced databases?

options.text: Synced databases allow you to make changes in Notion that reflect back in Asana, while imports are one-way.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Synced databases only go one direction (changes in Asana reflect in Notion). Imports are also one-time actions and do not sync back.

options.text: Importing duplicates your Asana data into Notion, while Synced databases connect the two tools without duplicating data.
options.explanation: This is correct. Importing creates a duplicate copy of the data in Notion, whereas Synced databases maintain a connection without duplicating the underlying data.

options.text: You cannot use Synced databases with Asana, only the import feature works.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. You can add a synced database from Asana by pasting a project link into a Notion page.

options.text: Synced databases automatically update Notion when you make changes in Asana, but imports require manual updates.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The Synced databases allow Asana changes to reflect in Notion. The import is a one-time action that does not automatically update.

questions.question: Before importing Asana projects into Notion, what is a requirement regarding your Asana team?

options.text: You must have an active paid subscription to Asana.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The article states that 'Any member on any plan can take advantage of the Asana importer.'

options.text: You must be the administrator of the Asana workspace.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. There is no mention of administrator requirements; just being associated with a team is sufficient.

options.text: You must be associated with a team in Asana, even if you are the only person on that team.
options.explanation: This is correct. The article explicitly notes that you must be associated with a team, even as a sole member, to import projects.

options.text: Your team must be invite-only to prevent unauthorized imports.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The article does not mention invite-only restrictions as a prerequisite.

questions.question: How are tasks imported if the assignee is not yet present in Notion?

options.text: The task will be assigned to the current user performing the import.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The task retains its original assignee, but represented as a placeholder.

options.text: The task will be unassigned and require manual assignment.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The user is added as a placeholder account to the workspace.

options.text: The user is added as a placeholder account to the workspace, which attributes comments and mentions.
options.explanation: This is correct. If the assignee does not exist in Notion, they are added as a placeholder to attribute comments and mentions.

options.text: The task is automatically skipped and left out of the import.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The task is imported, but the assignee is represented by a placeholder.

questions.question: Which Projects database view displays projects grouped by status (Planning, Backlog, On Track)?

options.text: Active (table view)
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The Active view shows projects that are to-do or in progress and filters out completed ones.

options.text: Timeline view
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The Timeline view displays project name, status, owner, and completion rate.

options.text: Board view
options.explanation: This is correct. The Board view shows projects grouped by status and sorted by descending priority.

options.text: All (table view)
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The All view displays all imported projects without the specific status grouping found in the Board view.

questions.question: What is the recommended limit for the number of tasks that should be imported per teamspace?

options.text: There is no limit; you can import as many tasks as you want.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. While there are no hard limits, the article recommends limiting imports to 20K tasks per teamspace.

options.text: A limit of 50K tasks per teamspace is recommended for optimal performance.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The recommended limit is 20K tasks.

options.text: 20K tasks per teamspace is the recommended limit.
options.explanation: This is correct. The article recommends limiting imports to 20K tasks per teamspace.

options.text: 10K tasks per workspace is the recommended limit.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The recommendation is specifically for tasks per teamspace.

questions.question: How does the Asana importer handle subtasks?

options.text: They are imported as separate tasks in the Tasks database.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The article states they are imported as subtasks within the same database.

options.text: They are discarded during the import process.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The article states they are imported as subtasks.

options.text: They are imported as subtasks (along with dependencies) that are within the same database.
options.explanation: This is correct. The Asana importer preserves subtasks and their dependencies within the same database.

options.text: They are promoted to top-level tasks in the Projects database.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The text explicitly says they are imported as subtasks within the same database.

questions.question: Which tool allows you to connect Asana to Notion without importing any data?

options.text: The /asana slash command
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The /asana slash command is used to start the import process.

options.text: Synced databases
options.explanation: This is correct. Synced databases connect Asana to Notion without importing data.

options.text: Projects & Tasks template
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The template is used to view the imported data, not to connect without importing.

options.text: Import logs
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Import logs are generated after the import is complete.

questions.question: What happens if you check the box next to your workspace name during the import process?

options.text: It excludes completed projects from the import.
options.explanation: This is correct. Checking the box allows you to exclude completed projects.

options.text: It includes only completed projects in the import.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Checking the box excludes completed projects.

options.text: It imports the entire Asana organization, not just the selected workspace.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The checkbox specifically relates to excluding completed projects within the selected workspace.

options.text: It syncs the projects in real-time with Notion.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. This feature is part of the import wizard, not a sync feature.

questions.question: Which Tasks database view is filtered to only show tasks assigned to you?

options.text: By Project (table view)
options.explanation: This is incorrect. This view shows all tasks grouped by project.

options.text: All tasks (table view)
options.explanation: This is incorrect. This view shows all imported tasks.

options.text: Mine (table view)
options.explanation: This is correct. The 'Mine' view is specifically filtered to show only tasks assigned to you.

options.text: Calendar view
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The Calendar view shows tasks by date, not by your assigned tasks specifically.

questions.question: Can you undo an import once it has been completed?

options.text: Yes, you can undo the import from the import log in the sidebar.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. There is no way to undo or preview the import once it has started or completed.

options.text: No, there is no way to preview or undo an import.
options.explanation: This is correct. The article states there is no way to preview or undo an import.

options.text: Yes, but only if the import is still in progress.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Once the import process begins, there is no undo mechanism.

options.text: No, but you can delete specific projects or tasks afterward.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. While you can delete specific projects or tasks after the import, you cannot undo the import action itself.

questions.question: What information does the Timeline view display for projects?

options.text: Project name, status, owner, and completion rate.
options.explanation: This is correct. The Timeline view displays project name, status, owner, and completion rate.

options.text: Task name, assignee, and status.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. That describes the Timeline view for *tasks*, not projects.

options.text: Project description and toggle content.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The description is found in a toggle on the project page, not in the Timeline view.

options.text: Grouped status and priority sorting.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. That describes the Board view for projects.

questions.question: Which property in Asana is mapped to the Status property in Notion during import?

options.text: The Section or Completed property in Asana.
options.explanation: This is correct. During the import process, you can choose to map the Notion Status property to either the Section or Completed property in Asana.

options.text: The Task Name property.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The Task Name is the title of the task, not a status property.

options.text: The Due Date property.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The Due Date is a date property, not a status property.

options.text: The Owner property.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The Owner is a person property, not a status property.

questions.question: What is true regarding the one-time nature of the import process?

options.text: Changes made in Asana will automatically update in Notion after the import.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The import is a one-time action, and changes in Asana after the import will not automatically update in Notion.

options.text: You can perform the import as many times as you want to sync data.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The import is a one-time action. To get updated data, you would need to re-run the import.

options.text: The import is a one-time action that does not maintain a connection with Asana.
options.explanation: This is correct. The article explicitly states that changes made in Asana after the import will not automatically update in Notion.

options.text: You must manually enter data into Notion after the import is complete.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The import process automatically moves the data from Asana into Notion.

questions.question: Which mapping does the Asana importer create between the two platforms?

options.text: Asana projects become Notion tasks, and Asana tasks become Notion projects.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Asana projects become Notion projects, and Asana tasks become Notion tasks.

options.text: Asana projects become Notion tasks, and Asana teams become Notion projects.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Asana projects map to Notion projects, and Asana teams map to Notion teamspaces.

options.text: Asana projects become Notion projects, and Asana tasks become Notion tasks.
options.explanation: This is correct. The article explicitly lists these mappings.

options.text: Asana organizations become Notion projects, and Asana workspaces become Notion tasks.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Organizations become Notion workspaces.
